<a id="class-canvasgroup"></a>

# CanvasGroup

**Inherits:** [Node2D](class_node2d.md#class-node2d) **<** [CanvasItem](class_canvasitem.md#class-canvasitem) **<** [Node](class_node.md#class-node) **<** [Object](class_object.md#class-object)

Merges several 2D nodes into a single draw operation.

## Description

Child [CanvasItem](class_canvasitem.md#class-canvasitem) nodes of a **CanvasGroup** are drawn as a single object. It allows to e.g. draw overlapping translucent 2D nodes without causing the overlapping sections to be more opaque than intended (set the [CanvasItem.self_modulate](class_canvasitem.md#class-canvasitem-property-self-modulate) property on the **CanvasGroup** to achieve this effect).

**Note:** The **CanvasGroup** uses a custom shader to read from the backbuffer to draw its children. Assigning a [Material](class_material.md#class-material) to the **CanvasGroup** overrides the built-in shader. To duplicate the behavior of the built-in shader in a custom [Shader](class_shader.md#class-shader), use the following:

```gdscript
shader_type canvas_item;
render_mode unshaded;

uniform sampler2D screen_texture : hint_screen_texture, repeat_disable, filter_nearest;

void fragment() {
    vec4 c = textureLod(screen_texture, SCREEN_UV, 0.0);

    if (c.a > 0.0001) {
        c.rgb /= c.a;
    }

    COLOR *= c;
}
```

**Note:** Since **CanvasGroup** and [CanvasItem.clip_children](class_canvasitem.md#class-canvasitem-property-clip-children) both utilize the backbuffer, children of a **CanvasGroup** who have their [CanvasItem.clip_children](class_canvasitem.md#class-canvasitem-property-clip-children) set to anything other than [CanvasItem.CLIP_CHILDREN_DISABLED](class_canvasitem.md#class-canvasitem-constant-clip-children-disabled) will not function correctly.

## Properties

| [float](class_float.md#class-float)   | [clear_margin](#class-canvasgroup-property-clear-margin)   | `10.0`   |
|---------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|----------|
| [float](class_float.md#class-float)   | [fit_margin](#class-canvasgroup-property-fit-margin)       | `10.0`   |
| [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool)      | [use_mipmaps](#class-canvasgroup-property-use-mipmaps)     | `false`  |

---

## Property Descriptions

<a id="class-canvasgroup-property-clear-margin"></a>

[float](class_float.md#class-float) **clear_margin** = `10.0` [🔗](#class-canvasgroup-property-clear-margin)

-  **set_clear_margin**(value: [float](class_float.md#class-float))
- [float](class_float.md#class-float) **get_clear_margin**()

Sets the size of the margin used to expand the clearing rect of this **CanvasGroup**. This expands the area of the backbuffer that will be used by the **CanvasGroup**. A smaller margin will reduce the area of the backbuffer used which can increase performance, however if [use_mipmaps](#class-canvasgroup-property-use-mipmaps) is enabled, a small margin may result in mipmap errors at the edge of the **CanvasGroup**. Accordingly, this should be left as small as possible, but should be increased if artifacts appear along the edges of the canvas group.

---

<a id="class-canvasgroup-property-fit-margin"></a>

[float](class_float.md#class-float) **fit_margin** = `10.0` [🔗](#class-canvasgroup-property-fit-margin)

-  **set_fit_margin**(value: [float](class_float.md#class-float))
- [float](class_float.md#class-float) **get_fit_margin**()

Sets the size of a margin used to expand the drawable rect of this **CanvasGroup**. The size of the **CanvasGroup** is determined by fitting a rect around its children then expanding that rect by [fit_margin](#class-canvasgroup-property-fit-margin). This increases both the backbuffer area used and the area covered by the **CanvasGroup** both of which can reduce performance. This should be kept as small as possible and should only be expanded when an increased size is needed (e.g. for custom shader effects).

---

<a id="class-canvasgroup-property-use-mipmaps"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **use_mipmaps** = `false` [🔗](#class-canvasgroup-property-use-mipmaps)

-  **set_use_mipmaps**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **is_using_mipmaps**()

If `true`, calculates mipmaps for the backbuffer before drawing the **CanvasGroup** so that mipmaps can be used in a custom [ShaderMaterial](class_shadermaterial.md#class-shadermaterial) attached to the **CanvasGroup**. Generating mipmaps has a performance cost so this should not be enabled unless required.
